# openai_project_rate_limits (Resource)

Deprecated. Manage existing project rate limits as one resource per project. Use openai_project_rate_limit instead; this resource will be removed in provider v1.0.0.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "openai_project_rate_limits" "example" {
  project_id  = "proj_123"
  rate_limits = { "gpt-4o" = { max_tokens_per_1_minute = 20 } }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `project_id` (String) Project whose rate limits are managed.
- `rate_limits` (Attributes Map) Rate limits keyed by public model name. Removing a key stops managing that model and does not reset its remote limits. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rate_limits))

<a id="nestedatt--rate_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rate_limits`

Optional:

- `batch_1_day_max_input_tokens` (Number)
- `max_audio_megabytes_per_1_minute` (Number)
- `max_images_per_1_minute` (Number)
- `max_requests_per_1_day` (Number)
- `max_requests_per_1_minute` (Number)
- `max_tokens_per_1_minute` (Number)

Read-Only:

- `rate_limit_id` (String) Remote rate-limit identifier resolved by the provider.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = openai_project_rate_limits.example
  id = "proj_123"
}
```
